identity.short_name: Centralizer dimension of a tensor-sum nilpotent in so(35)

problem_spec.summary: Use the Jacobson-Morozov sl2-model and Clebsch-Gordan decomposition to determine the Jordan partition of a tensor-sum nilpotent operator on an orthogonal tensor product space, then compute the dimension of its centralizer in so(U) from the partition via the gl-centralizer and the orthogonal correction term.
givens: V is a 7-dimensional complex vector space with a non-degenerate symmetric bilinear form.
W is a 5-dimensional complex vector space with a non-degenerate symmetric bilinear form.
A \in End(V) is nilpotent with one Jordan block of size 7 and is skew-symmetric w.r.t. the form on V.
B \in End(W) is nilpotent with one Jordan block of size 5 and is skew-symmetric w.r.t. the form on W.
U = V ⊗ W with the induced non-degenerate symmetric bilinear form Q.
so(U,Q) = {S \in End(U) : S is skew-symmetric w.r.t. Q}.
ask: Compute dim_C { S \in so(U,Q) : ST = TS }.

parameter(s).name: tensor_pattern
parameter(s).purpose: determines the diagonal sl2-action and hence the Jordan partition of T

parameter(s).name: nilpotent_block_sizes
parameter(s).domain: single Jordan block in each factor
parameter(s).purpose: forces A,B to be principal nilpotents (raising operators)

invariant(s).name: orthogonal_structure
invariant(s).statement: The symmetric non-degenerate forms on V and W induce a symmetric non-degenerate form Q on U=V⊗W, and T lies in so(U,Q).

invariant(s).name: sl2_structure
invariant(s).statement: By Jacobson-Morozov, A and B are raising operators of sl2-triples, so V≅ and W≅ as sl2-modules; with the diagonal action on U, the raising operator is T.

invariant(s).name: jordan_partition_of_T
invariant(s).statement: Clebsch-Gordan gives U≅⊕⊕⊕⊕, hence the Jordan partition of T is λ=(11,9,7,5,3).

invariant(s).name: orthogonal_centralizer_dimension_formula
invariant(s).statement: If λ is the Jordan partition of a nilpotent in so, then dim Z_so(T) = (Σ_k (λ'_k)^2 - o(λ))/2, where λ' is the conjugate partition and o(λ) is the number of odd parts of λ.

essential_assumption(s): Field is C (characteristic 0), so Jacobson-Morozov and complete reducibility of sl2-modules apply.
The invariant bilinear forms are symmetric (odd-dimensional irreps), so the sl2-actions land in so.
T is interpreted as the diagonal sl2 raising operator on U.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The sl2-module structures of V and W are uniquely determined by the principal nilpotent hypotheses. Clebsch-Gordan uniquely fixes the decomposition of U and thus the Jordan partition λ of T. The orthogonal centralizer dimension is a deterministic function of λ, hence the answer is unique.

technique(s).name: Jacobson-Morozov sl2-triple construction
technique(s).purpose: realize A and B as sl2 raising operators on irreducible orthogonal modules

theorem(s).name: Jacobson-Morozov theorem
theorem(s).role: embeds a nilpotent element into an sl2-triple

theorem(s).name: Clebsch-Gordan rule for sl2
theorem(s).role: decomposes tensor products of sl2 irreducibles

theorem(s).name: C[t]-module centralizer formula
theorem(s).role: gives dim C_gl(T) = Σ (λ'_k)^2 for nilpotent Jordan type λ

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Jordan canonical form as a C[t]-module decomposition

critical_trick(s): Interpret T as the diagonal sl2 raising operator on U (Jacobson-Morozov).
Read off Jordan block sizes from the irreducible sl2 decomposition (Clebsch-Gordan).
Use the orthogonal centralizer formula (gl centralizer minus odd-block correction, halved).

library(s).name: numpy
library(s).reason: optional modular rank computation to verify the centralizer dimension by linear constraints

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: optional exact symbolic verification of conjugate partition and sums of squares
